current_judgment: Two rounds of prompt hardening completed (2026-03-21). Architectural fix: adapter now uses Ollama system field for instruction prompt instead of concatenation. Prompt now includes full voice_consistency definition covering POV boundary, perceptual/epistemic access, psychic distance, and an explicit anchoring method. Results: schema_reliability 1.0 (10/10 valid envelopes across all tested runs). Tonal/register voice_consistency reliably scored. POV-boundary enforcement in scores is not reliably achievable with qwen2.5:14b — model names violations in findings but does not consistently penalize them at the score level. scene-07 (omniscient slip) now passes (0.60). scene-06 (reader address) and scene-04 (first-person intrusion) do not reach thresholds under any prompt variant tested. This is a model capability limit, not a prompt gap. POV score thresholds are not a viable gate for this model.
next_required_decision: Decide whether to pursue candidate_baseline with voice_consistency scoped to tonal/register consistency only (POV-boundary enforcement documented as out of scope for qwen2.5:14b), or defer baseline adoption pending evaluation of a more capable model. If proceeding: run full 5-scene baseline set against the updated prompt + system-field adapter to confirm no regressions on non-POV dimensions, then promote to candidate_baseline with the POV limitation documented in provenance_notes.
provenance_notes: Metrics derived from operator judgment across 5 eval scenes (run-2026-03-21-001 through run-2026-03-21-005). schema_reliability: 5/5 runs returned valid schema envelope. false_positive_rate: 0 clearly incorrect findings identified across all 5 runs. surface_detection_rate: 4/4 targeted style issues detected (scene-04 counted as detected despite dimension miscategorization). All metrics are operator-judged from a small 5-scene set. Not benchmark-derived. Not gate-eligible. metric_profile is set to detection_reasoning as the closest available value; style analysis identifies issues and reasons about them but is not a binary detection task.
